name: Epoch
one_liner: A focus timer that's beautiful enough to leave on screen.
emotional_north_star: From 'where did the last 3 hours go?' to 'I can see my focus happening.'

target_user.who: Knowledge workers who do focused work — developers, designers, writers, students
target_user.behavior: Already familiar with the Pomodoro technique. Have tried timer apps and found them either ugly or bloated.
target_user.trigger: Sitting down to do deep work and wanting structure without friction
target_user.need: A timer that respects their attention: starts fast, looks great on screen, gets out of the way

design_philosophy.aesthetic: Techno-futuristic, clean, shiny. Frosted glass, subtle glows, precise typography.
design_philosophy.references: Linear's design language meets a high-end watch face. Dieter Rams meets a spaceship.
design_philosophy.personality: Opinionated, atmospheric, crafted. Not a utility — a focus object.

competitive_position.gap: No web-first Pomodoro timer exists with premium, futuristic design. The category design bar is on the floor.
competitive_position.differentiator: Optimizes for presence — how it feels on your screen — not for features.

scope_decisions.decision: Atmospheric and opinionated (not utility, not themeable)

scope_decisions.reason: Sweet spot between ephemeral and full tracking

scope_decisions.decision: Subtle transition chime + optional browser notification
scope_decisions.reason: Essential for usability without competing with user's music

scope_decisions.decision: Full Pomodoro config set, no theme picker
scope_decisions.reason: Complete without diluting the locked-down aesthetic
Bold, futuristic, evokes a period of focused time
